name: Friendly Co-working at Brooklyn Meeting
description: Join Friends and friends-of-Friends for co-working at Brooklyn Meeting. Drop by any time. We supply chairs, tables, power, tea, coffee, snacks, and wifi.

name: Peer Support Group for Job Seekers
description: A mutually supportive space for job seekers to share experiences, encouragement, and practical strategies. This group helps members build resilience, confidence, and hope through shared journey.
short_name: Peer Support Group for Job Seekers

name: Bipoc Meetup
description: Join the BIPOC Climbing Collective for a night of bouldering, ropes, and fun at Gowanus. This event is for Black, Indigenous, People of Color to climb and build community.

name: The Phoenix
description: The Phoenix hosts sober climbing events for members and guests on the second Saturday of every month. The only requirement to attend is 48 hours of continuous sobriety. Friends and family are welcome.

name: Crux Queer Femme
description: Crux Queer Femme is a climbing event designed for individuals who identify as femme or have felt femme and want to climb. This event offers a welcoming space for all.

name: Exploration: LIVE!
description: A live recording of the popular podcast Exploration: LIVE! featuring comedic discussions.

name: Tastes of Chinatown: Neighborhood History and Food Tour
description: Enjoy delicious cuisine while learning the compelling stories of Chinatown’s history on this sensory tour featuring four savory dishes and one dessert.

name: Distillery Tour
description: Explore the production process of NYC's oldest whiskey distillery, from milling and mashing to fermentation and distilling. The tour includes a visit to the barrel room for a guided tasting of core spirits.

name: NYCR Craft Night
description: Bring your current crafting project and work on it with other artists, crafters, and makers of all kinds. Projects range from costumes and woodworking to electronics and visual arts.

name: Whiskey Tasting Class
description: Learn how to nose, taste, and evaluate whiskey like a pro during this expert-led session covering production and barrel aging. The class includes a flight of five premium hand-crafted whiskeys.

name: Family Bowl
description: A family-friendly holiday bowling session open to all ages with no cover charge.

name: Play at BCM
description: Come explore some of BCM's most-loved exhibits, including toddler-favorite Totally Tots and the miniature shops of World Brooklyn.

name: Brooklyn Navy Yard: Past, Present & Future
description: This permanent exhibition explores the Brooklyn Navy Yard's history from its 1801 founding to its modern role as an innovation hub. Visitors can view artifacts and historical narratives spread across three floors of a restored 1858 residence.

name: Pen Pals
description: This play tells the story of two women who have been best friends for fifty years through letters, featuring Melissa Gilbert and Veanne Cox. Five percent of ticket sales are donated to Susan G. Komen for breast cancer research.

name: Friday Nights
description: Experience one of NYC's premier rooftop nightlife events featuring three floors of music and dancing. Guests can enjoy French dining and a dynamic atmosphere with optional bottle service.

name: Reggaeton Fridays @ The DL
description: Dance to the best Reggaeton beats at this multi-level club event. The night features high-energy Latin music across three floors with table reservations available.

name: DIY Tapestry Weaving for Beginners
description: Learn how to dress a loom and create small tapestry-style weavings that can be used as coasters or wall hangings. Each student receives a simple homemade loom to keep.

name: Brunch Italiano!
description: Spend a Saturday afternoon rolling rigatoni and cutting pasta sheets using the chitarra. This hands-on festa includes northern Italian bites, an open wine bar, and lively music.

name: Dancehall Forever
description: A high-energy late-night dance party dedicated to the best of dancehall music. Get ready for deep bass and vibrant rhythms at this Brooklyn venue.

name: Rooftop Saturdays
description: Spend your Saturday night on a dynamic rooftop overlooking the city. This event promises a snapshot of the city's rhythm with premium bottle service and diverse music.

name: Community Dinner
description: On the last Sunday of each month volunteers prepare and serve a meal to whoever wants one. Free clothing and groceries are also available. Open to everyone. This is a compassionate outreach to the community.

name: Tarot 101: Learn How to Do Tarot Readings
description: Discover the meanings of all 78 cards in the deck and practice intuitive shuffling techniques to perform your own readings. This class includes a classic Rider-Waite Tarot deck and a bundle of sage.
short_name: Learn How to Do Tarot Readings

name: Writers Group
description: A collaborative space for writers to share their current projects and receive constructive feedback from peers. This group supports various genres and levels of experience.

name: Issues and Action Discussion Circle
description: A weekly gathering to discuss social justice issues and current events from an ethical perspective. Participants explore ways to take meaningful action in their communities.

name: Sundays in Italia, Filled Pasta Making!
description: A hands-on Sunday session focused on the art of making filled Italian pasta. Enjoy a relaxed atmosphere with great food, drinks, and the feeling of Italy in the air.
short_name: Sundays in Italia, Filled Pasta Making!

name: Morning Food Scrap Drop-off
description: Bring fruit, vegetable, and coffee ground scraps to be turned into nutrient-rich compost used throughout the park. Meat, fish, citrus, and compostable cutlery are not accepted.

name: Winter Workshops with A Rosie Day
description: Join A Rosie Day for cozy crafting, skating, and seasonal magic. This free workshop is open to all ages, even for those who do not wish to skate.

name: Cold Process Soap Making for Beginners
description: Learn how to safely make high-quality, lye-based bars of soap in your own kitchen using simple ingredients. This workshop covers lye safety, oil selection, and custom scents.
short_name: Cold Process Soap Making for Beginners

name: The Open Mic Downstairs @ Frigid New York
description: A supportive weekly open mic where performers get seven minutes to try anything from performance art and comedy to music and dance. The event focuses on building an attentive and welcoming community.

name: 222 Tuesdays
description: Visit the historical loft at 222 Bowery to browse artist-curated books, listen to records, and explore the famous Burroughs bedroom. This weekly open house is a space for music and conversation.

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Simple Loaf - Park Slope)
description: Transport surplus food from Simple Loaf in Park Slope to the CHiPS community pantry. Volunteers help ensure that high-quality food reaches those in need rather than going to waste.

name: Lightroom Essentials
description: Explore the fundamentals of Adobe Lightroom Classic for beginning photographers.

name: Video Editing with Premiere
description: Navigate the Adobe Premiere interface to import footage and edit a short video segment with transitions and effects.

name: Weekly 12-Step Online Meeting
description: Join other Friends in 12-step Recovery Programs at a weekly online meeting held every Wednesday morning.

name: Soul Records Laboratory
description: Join Soul in the Horn for "Soul Records Laboratory," a weekly music series featuring up-and-coming DJs. Dance to electrifying jams with views of the NYC skyline while enjoying cocktails and food.

name: Tuff Enuf Trivia with Owen Scott
description: Test your knowledge with Tuff Enuf Trivia hosted by Owen Scott.

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Brooklyn Bread - Carroll Gardens Location)
description: Help transport donated items from Brooklyn Bread's Carroll Gardens store to the CHiPS soup kitchen. Volunteers play a vital role in recovering surplus food for distribution to the local community.

name: Evening Food Scrap Drop-off
description: Bring fruit, vegetable, and coffee ground scraps to be turned into nutrient-rich compost used throughout the park. Meat, fish, citrus, and compostable cutlery are not accepted.

name: ZOO
description: A docufiction hybrid exploring the serious and surreal story of a Seattle-area zoophile community. This haunting film refuses to compromise style for headline urgency.

name: The Flying Luna Clipper
description: A curated screening at the collective-run microcinema Spectacle in Brooklyn, featuring rare and under-the-radar cult programming.

name: Salsa Thursdays
description: Get your salsa on for free every Thursday with a dance lesson from Marlene Veras followed by a full-on dance party. Music is provided by DJ Ray Suave and a live percussionist.

name: Game Center Playtest Thursdays
description: Bring your work-in-progress games, including digital projects, board games, or VR, to get feedback from fellow game makers. Open to all designers, players, and game enthusiasts.

name: New Sanctuary Organizing Meeting
description: Learn how to protect neighbors and develop ways to face threats to immigrant communities through court witnessing and accompaniment programs.

name: Kill the Moonlight
description: An underground cult classic screening at the Spectacle microcinema collective in Williamsburg.

name: Book a School Tour
description: Hands-on guided tours based on age-appropriate lesson plans for grades K-12, encouraging students to think critically and creatively about food. Offered Thursdays & Fridays.

name: Weeknights
description: Part of the collective-run microcinema's usual array of unique programming, showcasing the best of underground cinema.

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Roca De Seguridad Church / Paris Baguette)
description: Volunteers collect donated food from Paris Baguette at the Roca De Seguridad Church and deliver it to CHiPS. This effort helps reduce food waste and supports the local community meals program.

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Colson Patisserie - 9th St. Park Slope)
description: Visit Colson Patisserie on 9th Street to collect surplus baked goods for delivery to CHiPS. This role is essential for maintaining the community meals and pantry programs.

name: Friday Night Vibes at Time Out Market
description: Start your weekend off right at Time Out Market New York’s stunning rooftop. Friday Night Vibes gets the party going with tunes from DJ Stretch, specialty cocktails and bites from various kitchens.

name: Standing Slightly Outside: Franklin Furnace, 1976—2026
description: A gallery show marks the 50th anniversary of Franklin Furnace, a vital New York arts space and archive.

name: Friday Nights @ The Delancey Nightclub
description: Join the city’s lively night owls on the dance floor for an electrifying night of music and drinks. Experience one of the most dynamic nightclub atmospheres in the city.

name: Reggaeton Fridays @ The Delancey's Lower Level
description: Dive into a world of high-energy Latin vibes and pulsating beats in the venue's lower level. This event is a go-to for Reggaeton fans looking for an unforgettable night out.

name: RuPaul’s Drag Race Watch Party Hosted by Lailah Lancing
description: Join host Lailah Lancing for a weekly fierce watch party featuring big screens, themed cocktails, and tacos. Participate in an interactive prediction game for a chance to win free drinks and prizes.
short_name: RuPaul’s Drag Race Watch Party Hosted by Lailah Lancing

name: Evento's Pasta Making Dance Party!
description: Master regional Italian pasta making in a high-energy setting with an open wine bar and a live DJ. Guests can choose to take their handmade pasta home or donate it to a local organization.

name: Nyc-Dsa 101
description: New to NYC-DSA or maybe just socialist-curious? Learn about the organization's structure, goals, and how you can get involved in local struggles.

name: Wake Up & Dance with Suzi Shelton
description: Award-winning children's musician Suzi Shelton leads a joyful concert series for young dancers and their families.

name: The Best of Me
description: Part of the Best of Spectacle 2025 series, highlighting top underground and rare films screened at the microcinema.

name: Despiser
description: A late-night cult screening at Brooklyn's collective-run microcinema, exploring the fringes of independent cinema.

name: Spotlight Saturdays: Noise Complaint
description: DJ LSJ presents a late-night party featuring hip-hop, RnB, Afro beats, and Latin mixes. Enjoy drink specials from Cazadores Tequila and jello shots on the rooftop.

name: Spotlight Saturdays with DJ LSJ
description: Celebrate Saturday night with live DJ sets by DJ LSJ, featuring specialty drinks and great views of the NYC skyline.

name: Saturday Nights @ The Delancey Nightclub
description: Immerse yourself in the ultimate Saturday night experience with pulsating music and a lively crowd. It is the perfect destination for an epic evening of dancing and drinks.

name: Safaera Saturdays @ The Delancey's Lower Level
description: Experience a night of non-stop energy featuring the best in Latin and Reggaeton music. Get lost in the rhythm and enjoy the drinks in a vibrant underground setting.

name: Tom D's Big New York Show
description: Comedian and certified NYC tour guide Tom Delgado leads an entertaining tour through different aspects of New York history. The show features special guests, trivia with prizes, and storytelling.
short_name: Tom D's Big New York Show

name: Femmes Book Club
description: A book club for femme-presenting readers discussing "BITTER SWEET" by Hattie Williams and other contemporary fiction featuring badass characters.

name: Brooklyn Navy Yard: World War II Tour
description: Explore the history of the Navy Yard during its peak production years in World War II. This walking tour visits historical sites and shares stories of the sailors and workers who served there.
short_name: Brooklyn Navy Yard: World War II Tour

name: The Fat Cats Youth Orchestra Open Workshop and Rehearsals
description: Weekly open workshops and rehearsals for the Fat Cats Youth Orchestra, focusing on mentorship and musical excellence.
short_name: The Fat Cats Youth Orchestra Open Workshop and Rehearsals

name: Free One Hour Outdoor Yoga in La Plaza
description: A welcoming multi-level yoga class open to everyone in an outdoor setting. This session focuses on mindfulness, body breath awareness, and stretching under the trees.
short_name: Free One Hour Outdoor Yoga in La Plaza

name: Silent Vigil for Peace and Non-Violence
description: Join Friends at this silent vigil for peace and nonviolence held at the Washington Square Arch on the first Sunday of the month.
short_name: Silent Vigil for Peace and Non-Violence

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Brooklyn French Bakers - Park Slope Location)
description: Volunteer to pick up bread and pastry donations from Brooklyn French Bakers in Park Slope. These rescued items are delivered to CHiPS to support their soup kitchen and community outreach programs.

name: Murder, They Read Book Club
description: Join fellow readers to dissect murder mysteries and thrillers, currently discussing "THE ONCE AND FUTURE ME" by Melissa Pace.

name: Short(er) Slavic Novels Book Club with Arya
description: This meeting focuses on Ivan Klima's "Love and Garbage," a novel set in Prague that explores love and conscience under totalitarianism. This book club focuses on Slavic literature under 300 pages.

name: First Mondays: Readings of New Works In Progress
description: Organized by Sarah Schulman, this monthly program features writers from various generations previewing new works-in-progress devoid of censorship.
short_name: Readings of New Works In Progress

name: Romance Book Club
description: A monthly meeting for romance enthusiasts to discuss swoon-worthy stories, featuring "VIOLET THISTLEWAITE IS NOT A VILLAIN ANYMORE" by Emily Krempholtz.

name: Orientation
description: The first step for anyone interested in BRIC's media classes and community resources.

name: Quiet Reading Brooklyn
description: Spend one hour reading in silence followed by one hour of socializing with fellow book lovers.

name: Literary Black Book Club with Genay
description: The group will discuss "The Wilderness" by Angela Flournoy, which follows five Black women through twenty years of friendship. This club focuses on contemporary fiction primarily written by Black women.

name: Schmoedipus
description: A curated screening at Spectacle, showcasing under-the-radar and unique independent programming in Brooklyn.

name: Arctic Reverie
location: The Sheen Center for Thought & Culture
description: Artist Leslie Sobel presents paintings documenting the beauty and fragility of the High Arctic based on residencies in Svalbard. The works aim to capture the sacred nature of landscapes transforming due to climate change.

name: Taking Inspiration from The Best American Short Stories with Cora Lewis
description: Explore the craft of short fiction by analyzing selections from The Best American Short Stories to find creative inspiration.
short_name: Taking Inspiration from The Best American Short Stories

name: Close Encounters: Writing Intimacy and Connection with Danielle Lazarin
description: This workshop focuses on the art of writing authentic emotional and physical intimacy in fiction through craft exercises.

name: Trivia @ Parklife Hosted by Hope Morawa
description: Test your knowledge at this free weekly trivia night hosted by Hope Morawa. Compete for fun prizes including free drinks, tacos, and tickets to littlefield.

name: Fundamentals of Screenwriting
description: Learn the basic elements of structure, format, and storytelling techniques for writing your first screenplay.

name: Video Production in the Field
description: Gain hands-on experience using professional-grade digital video cameras, sound equipment, and lighting in this certification course.

name: Open for Bowling!
description: Midweek bowling session open to all ages for evening lanes and food.

name: PACKRAT: The Quest por la Abundancia
description: A visually crafted puppet-forward play that explores themes of home, belonging, and responsibility during the climate crisis. The production is inspired by the Indigenous Comcáac people of northern Mexico.
short_name: PACKRAT: The Quest por la Abundancia

name: DJ Nights at Skate
description: Join DJ girlcrush and guest DJ RAISA for a night of high-energy music at the ice rink. These sessions feature a mix of house, techno, and hyperpop to keep you moving on and off the ice.

name: Silver Arrow Band
description: A high-energy live music showcase from party starters designed to help you plan your next event.

name: Winter Virtual Poetry Intensive: Amateur Poetics
location: The Poetry Society of New York
description: A six-week poetry workshop exploring the concept of 'amateur' poetics, focusing on writing for pleasure and interest rather than profit.

name: Everyday Hauntings: Writing Contemporary Gothic Fiction with JD Scott
description: Examine elements of modern Gothic fiction and learn how to incorporate uncanny atmospheres into contemporary settings.

name: Writing Short Fiction: Where to Begin with Amy Silverberg
description: An introductory workshop designed to help writers find their voice and start crafting short stories covering character, plot, and prose.

name: Yoga & Wellness Session
description: Join a community wellness session where attendees are encouraged to bring their own mat. Walk-ins are welcome with credit card payment at the door.

name: Fantasy & Fables Book Club
description: A recurring monthly club focusing on the fantasy and romantasy genres, specifically highlighting debut novels. Attendees can enjoy a discount on drinks at the bar during the discussion.

name: The Diary
description: A compelling theatrical exploration of personal narratives and secrets revealed through the pages of a diary.

name: DetoNation Rat Cabaret
description: An edgy and avant-garde cabaret performance featuring music, comedy, and experimental theatrical elements.

name: "Sima" and "E.G.: A Musical Portrait of Emma Goldman"
description: A double feature including a musical portrait of the famous anarchist and activist Emma Goldman.
short_name: A Musical Portrait of Emma Goldman"

name: Open Hours at the Environmental Education Center
description: Explore the park's ecology through a 250-gallon aquarium, a scale model of the park, and hands-on crafts. Educators are available to help reveal the hidden stories of the Brooklyn waterfront.

name: Community Access Thursdays
description: Explore the Museum's most-loved exhibits during these community access hours held every Thursday afternoon.

name: New Book Book Club with Sarah
description: This month the club discusses David Szalay's 2025 Booker Prize-winning novel "Flesh." This event is currently listed as sold out.

name: No Sex Last Night (Double Blind)
description: A screening of Sophie Calle and Greg Shephard's experimental road movie at Brooklyn's Spectacle microcinema.

name: Yamavicascope: the Short Films of Isao Yamada
description: A retrospective of short films by Isao Yamada, showcasing the artist's unique visual style and poetic storytelling.
short_name: the Short Films of Isao Yamada

name: Themed Thursdays hosted by Milk
description: Join drag performer and figure skater Milk for a themed night on the ice with curated weekly themes. The best on-theme costume wins a pair of SKATE admissions, and Milk performs at 7:30 pm.

name: Stop Motion Film & Miniatures
description: View 'The Photobooth Technician', a stop-motion film by Rachel Rowe that celebrates the art of the photobooth. The exhibition also features intricate miniatures created by the puppetry artist.

name: The New York Neo-Futurists Present: Best of the Infinite Wrench
description: A fast-paced collection of short plays that attempt to perform 30 plays in 60 minutes, blending high-concept art with low-brow humor.

name: Studio Podcasting
description: Learn the fundamentals of producing a podcast in a professional studio, covering microphone setup and console settings.

name: Prospect Park Board Gaming Club
description: An evening dedicated to tabletop gaming and social strategy. Players of all skill levels are welcome to join for a night of board games and community fun.

name: The Passing
description: A midnight-adjacent screening of Bill Viola's experimental work exploring the boundaries between life and death.

name: Wto/99
description: An immersive archival documentary reanimating the street protests of the 1999 Seattle WTO summit. Includes a Q&A with Editor/Producer Alex Megaro.

name: Hello Dankness
description: A curated screening by the Spectacle microcinema collective in Brooklyn, showcasing unique and under-the-radar independent programming.

name: 2024 Baby Meetup at Wild East
description: A casual weekly Friday meetup for parents, families, and babies at Wild East Brewing.

name: Old Yorkers
description: A sketch and improv show featuring performers bringing unique New York perspectives to the stage.

name: All That Fall
description: Directed by Joanne Akalaitis, this experimental production of Samuel Beckett’s first play with a female protagonist creates a visual and sonic landscape of ordinary life saturated with morbid humor. The performance follows Maddie Rooney's journey to a rail station as she encounters various characters and reflects on decline and death.

name: Pianos: Free Entry before 11PM w/ RSVP
description: A late-night DJ and dance event at the Pianos Main Bar with free entry for guests who RSVP before 11 PM.

name: Street Food City
description: This exhibition celebrates generations of immigrant street food entrepreneurs in New York City and the diverse dishes they introduced to the culinary landscape.

name: Intro to Radio Podcasting
description: Hands-on experience with professional audio recorders and editing software to create your own podcast segment.

name: The Art of Bonsai
description: Learn the ancient art form of growing miniature trees in containers from a bonsai professional. Students take home their own bonsai tree, container, and soil after learning maintenance and design techniques.

name: Child of Peach
description: A wild fantasy screening at Spectacle, exploring the boundaries of cult world cinema.

name: The Animations of Le Dernier Cri
description: A program of wild, graphic animations from the Marseille-based art collective Le Dernier Cri.
short_name: The Animations of Le Dernier Cri

name: Live Music Performance
description: An evening of live music featuring talented artists at the Bowery Ballroom.

name: The Mic at The Second City
description: An open mic event where new and established comedians test out their latest material.

name: Open Level Flying Trapeze
description: Participants of all skill levels, ages 5 to adult, can experience the thrill of flight in this open-level trapeze class. Professional instructors guide students through foundational flying and acrobatic techniques in a supportive environment.

name: Coffee with Comrades
description: Informal social meetups held across different NYC neighborhoods. Meet fellow socialists, discuss local issues, and build community in a relaxed setting.

name: St. John's Watch Party
description: Watch the St. John's Red Storm take on Providence, Creighton, and Villanova. Enjoy drink specials on Game Theory, the official pilsner of St. John's Athletics, while you cheer on the Johnnies.

name: Matinee Disco @ Joyface
description: An afternoon disco dance party at Joyface featuring retro beats and a nostalgic vibe.

name: Accelerated TV Studio
description: Learn essential skills for television studio environments, including camera operation, lighting setup, and live broadcasting. This certification course is designed for individuals with some production background.

name: Transit Tots
description: This early morning program for families with children ages 18 months to 3 years features story-time and crafts. It is designed to engage the youngest transportation fans through imaginative play.

name: Schneider Concerts Presents: Ulysses Quartet
description: The Ulysses Quartet celebrates 10 years after their New York debut with a program of Debussy and Mendelssohn. The concert includes a world premiere of Tina Davidson's "Just Before Light."

name: Sunday Worship Service
description: Join a joyful and caring religious community for weekly worship services that inspire spiritual growth and justice. Services feature preaching by Rev. Micah C. Ma and others, and are available in-person or via Facebook Live, YouTube, and Zoom.

name: Live Music Sundays at Time Out Market
description: Join the Signature Sundays lineup for live music every week from 2-5pm. Enjoy performances from a rotating schedule of artists like Melaner, Yannie, and Najah Lewis during your weekend brunch.

name: Live Music: Tiny Montgomery Series
description: A rotating lineup of live music performances in our intimate Tiny Montgomery space, featuring jazz, folk, and indie artists like Bessie and the Rainbowkids, Noah Rott, and Sunjae Lee.

name: [Parlor Event] MTG Casual Commander
description: Join fellow Magic: The Gathering players for casual Commander games in the Parlor. This is a social, non-competitive format focused on fun decks and community interaction.

name: [Gallery Event] Creative Jam
description: A collaborative space for creators to work on game design, art, or writing projects. Bring your current work-in-progress and get feedback or inspiration from fellow enthusiasts.

name: Anatol’s Camera + Rare Photos
description: View rare photographs of Anatol Josepho, the inventor of the photobooth, and an original camera from 1925. This display provides an intimate look at the origin of analog photography technology.

name: Celebrity Prints
description: Discover one-of-a-kind celebrity photostrips featuring stars such as Michael Jackson and Serena Williams. These candid snapshots capture a unique, analog perspective on fame and identity.

name: The Photobooth Technicians Project
description: An exhibit featuring a unique accordion-fold book that explores photobooth history through interviews with 49 global technicians. The project highlights the manual labor and culture that keeps vintage booths running.

name: Photoshop Essentials
description: Learn to use Adobe Photoshop for photo enhancement, typography, and graphic design in this beginner-friendly course.

name: Vladimir Nabokov Book Club with Arya
description: The group begins a chronological journey through Vladimir Nabokov's work, starting with his first novel, "Mary." The discussion will examine the author's precise prose and emotional depth.

name: Concern for Quaker Living (CQL) Meeting
description: Working group focused on community wellness, housing feasibility, and nurturing a caring community for NYC Friends in all stages of life.

name: Writing a Coming-of-Age Novel with August Thompson
description: August Thompson leads a workshop focused on the specific challenges and tropes of the Bildungsroman genre and character arcs.

name: Adult Acrobatics
description: Designed for adults of all experience levels, this class focuses on floor-based acrobatics and dynamic movement. It emphasizes building core strength, flexibility, and coordination through various gymnastic and physical theater skills.

name: InspiredWordNYC Presents: Open Mic – Music / Poetry / Comedy
description: A long-running weekly open mic showcase featuring music, poetry, and comedy talent from across the city.

name: Monday Indie Comedy
description: A showcase featuring independent comedic talent and rising stars from the local scene.

name: Masterpiece Mondays
description: Each Monday, participants learn about a new artist and create their own masterpieces inspired by their work. This program encourages teens to try new techniques and use their imagination to create unique art projects.

name: Monday Night Bingo
description: A rowdy weekly bingo competition where participants can win bar tabs and prizes. Every drink order includes a ticket for a free personal pizza cooked in the wood-fired oven.

name: Bored of Dating Apps Singles Night
description: A popular offline social event for singles to connect over books, wine, and coffee without dating apps. The evening features early and late sessions for socializing in a relaxed bookshop atmosphere.
short_name: Bored of Dating Apps Singles Night

name: Public Hearing
description: A monthly gathering where the public can voice concerns during the hearing followed by the general board meeting.

name: Mary Beth’s New Joke Night
description: Mary Beth hosts an evening dedicated to testing out fresh material and new jokes in an intimate comedy setting.

name: Goat Milk Soap with Mix-Ins
description: Create luxurious bars of goat's milk soap using a cold-process method designed to prevent scorching. Students will customize their soaps with textures and colors using ingredients like coffee and mica powders.

name: God Told Me to (on 16Mm)
description: Larry Cohen’s 1976 cult classic horror-thriller screened on 16mm film for a unique analog viewing experience.

name: Writing a New York Novel with Rob Franklin
description: Focus on the city as a character and learn how to capture the unique elements and essence of New York-based fiction.

name: Strength & Stability
description: This fitness session targets core strength and overall body stability to improve athletic performance and daily wellness. The class utilizes specialized movement techniques designed to enhance balance, physical resilience, and core control.

name: House of SpeakEasy: Seriously Entertaining
description: A literary cabaret series bringing together writers for a fast-paced night of original storytelling connected by unique monthly themes.

name: Tuesday Comedy
description: Mid-week laughs featuring a diverse rotation of professional comedians and special guests.

name: Trivia Night
description: Test your knowledge across multiple rounds of general trivia to win prizes and glory. Like all events here, your drink purchase comes with a complimentary personal pizza.

name: Tuesday Night Jazz with Danny Jonokuchi
description: Enjoy an intimate evening of jazz and wine with Danny Jonokuchi & The Revisionists every other Tuesday night. Hear award-winning swing tunes and dance the night away in the Wine Cave.

name: In The Living Room with Daphne Always & Gray Caro
description: Join Daphne Always, Gray Caro, and a fantastic array of friends bringing various disciplines and media to the stage, followed by an open mic session.

name: Angelika Members Half-Price Tuesday
description: Exclusively for Angelika Members, enjoy 50% off movie tickets every Tuesday, alongside special concessions deals like 'Tuesday Twice as Sweet'.

name: Evil Trivia
description: Put your knowledge to the test at our weekly trivia night held every Tuesday evening. Enjoy authentic European beer and food while competing for prizes in the heart of Williamsburg.

name: Open Mic hosted by Brian Acosta Arya
description: A weekly offsite open mic session hosted by Brian Acosta Arya, providing a stage for poets and performers at the Loisaida Center.
short_name: Open Mic hosted by Brian Acosta Arya

name: Introduction to Homebuying Workshop
description: Join a special session with Nanette Jimenez from Webster Bank to explore the homebuying process and housing counselor support.

name: Reading for the Craft Book Club
description: This meeting features a discussion of Souvankham Thammavongsa’s novel "Pick a Color," focused on storytelling craft. Participants will analyze perspective choices, plot beats, and sentence-level sonics.
short_name: Reading for the Craft Book Club

name: Legendary Locals Featuring the Bmk House Band – Open Mic
description: Every Wednesday, join the BMK house band on stage to perform your favorite songs in a professional live venue setting.
short_name: Legendary Locals Featuring the Bmk House Band – Open Mic

name: Luma Yoga and Movement Workshop
description: A wellness session focused on yoga and movement where participants can register in advance or attend as walk-ins. This community session focuses on mindfulness and physical practice.

name: Creative Rebels JAM
description: An open jam session for experimental and creative musicians to collaborate and explore new sounds. This event provides a welcoming space for artistic improvisation and networking.

name: Tech Action General Biweekly Meeting
description: A biweekly gathering for tech workers to organize around labor rights, technology policy, and socialist principles. Open to anyone interested in the intersection of tech and politics.

name: 99 Minutes or Less
description: A fast-paced musical showcase where artists are given limited time to shine. Expect a diverse range of genres and high energy.

name: 3D Modeling with Blender
description: Discover techniques to navigate Blender software, create 3D objects, and work with virtual lighting and cameras. Skills learned apply to post-production filmmaking, art, and motion graphics workflows.

name: Drink 'N Draft: Creative Writing Workshop
description: A laptop-free handwriting workshop that uses visual prompts to help writers of all levels discover new creative directions. The evening includes dedicated writing time and an optional sharing session.

name: [Gallery Event] Mini Painting

description: Author Jessica Walker and editor Kelsey Murphy discuss the creative and publishing process behind the YA graphic novel "The Secret Astronomers." The talk explores how themes of friendship and grief are woven into an astronomy context.
short_name: Secrets of a YA Graphic Novel

name: Climate Cafe & Winter Poetry
description: An evening of climate conversation paired with winter-themed poetry and musical elements.

name: Michael Nau + Magic Tuber String Band
description: Singer-songwriter Michael Nau brings his indie-folk sensibilities to the Gowanus stage.
short_name: Michael Nau + Magic Tuber String Band

name: Jessie J - No Secrets Tour
description: The powerhouse vocalist performs her global pop hits and soul-stirring new material on her latest tour.

name: Good Girls Gone Bad: Contemporary Novels of Morally Questionable Women with Hilary Davidson
description: Hilary Davidson leads an exploration of contemporary novels featuring morally complex and questionable female protagonists.
short_name: Contemporary Novels of Morally Questionable Women

name: Tenement Talk: Preparing for the Immigration Journey
description: Historians Kevin Kenny and Maddalena Marinari discuss the rituals and traditions of Italian and Irish immigrants as they prepared to leave their homelands. The talk focuses on the physical objects people carried for protection and the customs surrounding departure and arrival.

name: CB3 Veterans Services Committee Meeting
description: A meeting dedicated to identifying and addressing the needs of veterans living within the community board's district.

name: One Way Up | Vice Club | Seamstress
description: Catch a night of electric guitar-driven performances by One Way Up, Vice Club, and Seamstress.
short_name: One Way Up | Vice Club | Seamstress

name: Lectures on Tap - The Birth, Life, and Death of the Sun
description: An overview of the lifecycle of our nearest star, exploring the nuclear processes that power life on Earth and the Sun's eventual end.
short_name: Lectures on Tap - The Birth, Life, and Death of the Sun

name: Lectures on Tap - Existential Courage: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: A philosophical examination of Douglas Adams' iconic series and its unique approach to the meaning of life through humor.
short_name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

name: Standing Above the Clouds
description: The movement to protect Mauna Kea through the intergenerational stories of women in three Native Hawaiian families. Followed by a Q&A with director Jalena Keane-Lee.

name: Souren Baronian Quartet
description: Souren Baronian, a master of both jazz and Middle Eastern idioms, performs with his quartet. The show features a sophisticated blend of scales and improvisation.

name: Desert Sharks, Wetsuit, Shaggo
description: A high-octane rock night featuring the garage-punk energy of Desert Sharks, Wetsuit, and Shaggo.

name: Studying the Sultry Genre of Romance Novels
description: A deep dive into the evolution and social significance of romance novels with Stef Ferrari. This session explores classic and modern romance tropes and why the genre remains satisfying for readers today.
short_name: Studying the Sultry Genre of Romance Novels

name: Virtual Information Session for First Year Students
description: Learn about the value of a St. Joseph's education, including generous financial aid packages and innovative academic offerings. This session is specifically designed for high school students and prospective first-year undergraduates.
short_name: Virtual Information Session for First Year Students

name: Bright Light Bright Light: Not Like All The Other Boys
description: Rod Thomas presents a rollercoaster ride through his catalogue of joyful, 80s/90s influenced dance pop and torch songs.
short_name: Not Like All The Other Boys

name: Immutable Screening Registration
description: Screening of a documentary following students from the Washington Urban Debate League as they fight to find their voices.

name: Down & Out presented by the Exponential Festival
description: Down&out is a new performance series about feeling bad at the club. This work is presented as part of the Exponential Festival.
short_name: Down & Out presented by the Exponential Festival

name: Kalia Vandever Quartet
description: Trombonist Kalia Vandever kicks off her residency with a quartet featuring Zekkereya El-magharbel, Micah Thomas, and Kweku Sumbry. The group explores contemporary jazz through the unique lens of dual trombones.

name: Films from the Burmese Resistance
description: A screening of short films reflecting on 5 years since the Myanmar Coup and the ongoing resistance.

name: Sara Levine: The Hitch w/ Roxane Gay
description: A delightfully unhinged comedy following a woman as she attempts to exorcise the spirit of a dead corgi from her nephew.

name: School of Rock NYC Grad Bands
description: Witness the next generation of rock talent as the School of Rock NYC Grad Bands showcase their skills across two nights.
short_name: School of Rock NYC Grad Bands

name: Women in Food Panel: Ali Rosen & Special Guests
description: Bestselling author Ali Rosen hosts a panel of special guests to discuss her new book, The Slow Burn, and women in the food industry.

name: Natti Vogel's Den of Gay Sex & Even Gayer Feelings
description: Natti Vogel shares stories of autonomy, pleasure, and transcendence through a muscular and flexible body of musical work.
short_name: Natti Vogel's Den of Gay Sex & Even Gayer Feelings

name: Glow Up Con Intencion
description: A wellness and community-focused workshop centered around self-care and setting meaningful intentions.

name: Pisha
description: Emerging artist Pisha brings their unique musical style and captivating presence to the Baby's All Right stage.

name: BKLYN Leadership Summit
description: A professional summit focused on Brooklyn's community leadership and development. Attendees can connect with local leaders and participate in discussions about thriving culture.

name: Slavery in Plain Sight: A 19th Century Merchant’s Home
description: This special exhibition highlights New York City's deep economic involvement in the slave trade through items like mahogany, sugar, and cotton. It examines how prominent merchant families profited from the slave economy even after abolition in New York State.

name: Blu Jay, Miranda Tonsetic, Molly Sierra
description: A showcase of emerging talent featuring Blu Jay, Miranda Tonsetic, and Molly Sierra.
short_name: Blu Jay, Miranda Tonsetic, Molly Sierra

name: Chris Redd's A Comedy Show!
description: Comedian Chris Redd hosts an evening of stand-up comedy at Caveat. The show features a mix of high-energy humor and special guest performers.

name: Rush
description: Prepare a five-minute story about a time you were in a hurry, felt an adrenaline surge, or experienced the thrill of the chase. This open-mic StorySLAM invites anyone to share a true, personal story on the night's theme.

name: Not of This Earth
description: A late-night session of experimental and atmospheric music.

name: Hypertension and Global Health
description: A presentation addressing hypertension as a leading cause of preventable mortality within a global health framework.

name: Horseshoe Theory vs The Final Boss: A Pokémon Improv Showdown!
description: A wild improv team appears in this Pokémon-themed battle. It's a competitive improv showdown inspired by the world of pocket monsters.

name: This MY Brother
description: Tallie Medel and Julian Hernandez lead an experimental clown experience featuring live-directing elements. It is an exploration of physical comedy and spontaneous performance.

name: Peter Calo Returns
description: Acclaimed singer-songwriter and guitarist Peter Calo returns to the Pangea stage for a night of acoustic storytelling and melody.

name: The New York Philosophy Club: Clinton Hill
description: A philosophical discussion gathering at the Brooklyn Masonic Temple, bringing together diverse perspectives for rounds of group dialogue.

name: Transit Techies #36: Colin Miller (Subway Builder) & Greg Feliu (The Busiest Subway Lines in NYC, Ranked)
description: A transit-focused meetup featuring Colin Miller discussing his realistic Subway Builder simulation game and Greg Feliu presenting data-driven rankings of NYC subway line ridership. This hybrid event includes talks followed by mingling with light bites and refreshments.

name: Wurst Wednesdays: Hot Dogs in Brooklyn
description: Indulge in a variety of hot dog and sausage specials every Wednesday. These savory treats are paired with rotating draft lines in a cozy bar atmosphere.
short_name: Wurst Wednesdays: Hot Dogs in Brooklyn

name: Block Printing for Beginners
description: Learn the fundamentals of relief printing and carving to create your own unique designs. This hands-on workshop is specifically designed for beginners and covers everything from transferring designs to printing on paper and fabric.
